sutta: once when the exalted 1 was dwelling on mount valdez peak near raja gaha the verbal ananda approached him saluted and sat down at 1 side so seated he said to the exalted 1 lord 6 breeds have been declared by purana kasapa the black the blue the red the yellow the white and the purest white lord here is the black breed declared by him mutton butchers pork butchers fowlers hunters thugs fishermen robbers cutthroats jailers and all others who follow a bloody trade here is the blue breed monks who live as though with the thorn in the side and all others who profess the deed and doing here is the red james and the loin cloth full here's the yellow white white robe householders and followers of naked ascetics here is the white fakers f-a-k-i-r-s and their disciples and here lord is the breed of the purest white declared by purana kasapa nanda vacca and makali lord these are the 6 breeds declared by him i just stopped here for a moment to comment this purana kasapa is 1 of the well-known leaders of the external ascetics and there were 6 during the time of the buddha who were quite well known purana kasapa was 1 of them and all these 6 had wrong views and uh here he's saying that there are 6 types of persons and he mentioned the blue the monks who live as though with a thorn in the side and all others who profess the deed and doing that means those who believe in kamala doing the action or the deed is karma but like this person probably does not believe in gamma um and then james and the loincloth form jeans are those naked ascetics and but they put a small little loincloth to cover their private partner and these are the jeans there are some others who are totally naked but the jeans just use a small cloth to recover and then he mentions another tree nanda these are all with wrong views then the buddha said but what ananda does the whole world agree with purana kasapa in his declaration of his in this declaration of his ananda replied certainly not lord well ananda just as men might thrust a piece of meat on some poor needy unwilling wretch saying here sir eat this meat and pay for it too even so is purana kasapa's declaration to these recluses and brahmins made without their consent as though by a foolish witness namskaar lacking common sense just stop here to comment again so you see here the buddha calls this person a foolish witness nam skull lacking common sense so sometimes the buddha can also use some strong words then buddha continue i verily ananda will declare the 6 breeds here give give heed i will speak yes lord repeat rejoin the variable ananda and the exalted 1 said and what ananda are the 6 breeds there are some of black breed ananda who breed black dhamma some of black breed who breed white dharma some are black breed who breed nirvana neither black nor white there are some a white breed who breed white dhamma some of white breed who breed black dhamma and some a white breed who breed nibana neither black nor white and how ananda breached the blackberry black dhamma consider ananda 1 reborn in a low caste clan barya hunter weaver wheel right sweeper in a poor family where food and drink are scarce life is hard clothing and livelihood hard to come by he is ugly ill-featured misshapen and much afflicted being blind or deformed in hand or lame or crippled and there is and is no recipient of food drink clothes carriages flowers scents ointments bed lodging or lighting and suppose he uh wavers in the wrong way in deed thought and word on the breaking up of the body after death he arises in the wayward way the ill way the abyss hell thus ananda some of the black breed breed black dharma and how anandam breached the black breed white dharma consider again 1 born in a low caste clan etc that means paria hunter weaver will ride sweeper in a poor family where food and drink are scarce life is hard etc who walks in the right way indeed word and thought on the breaking up of the body after death he arises in the good way the heaven world thus ananda some of the black breed breed white dhamma and how ananda breathes the black breed nibana neither black nor white consider again 1 born in a low caste family paria hunter weaver wheel ride with sweeper etc ugly huge featured misshapen 1 who has his hair um who has his hair and beard shaved dawns the yellow rope and goes forth from the home to the homeless light thus gone forth he rids himself of the 5 hindrances which weakens the mental uh weakens the mental defilements by insight becomes firmly fixed in the 4 in 10 states of mindfulness makes become the 7 factors of enlightenment as they are and breeze nibana neither black nor white doesn't under some of the black breed breed nibana neither black nor white and how ananda breeds the white breed black dhamma consider 1 born in a high caste clan noble or brahmin or householder owning stately homes riches wealth domains with gold and silver in plenty means and service in plenty corn and grain in plenty is well formed sightly pleasing blessed with a lily-like loveliness is a recipient of food drink clothes carriages flowers sands ointments bed lodging and lighting and suppose he walked in the right way in deed word and thought on the breaking up of the body after death he arises sorry and suppose he along in the wrong way indeed word and thought on the breaking up of the body after death he arises in hell does ananda some of the white breed breed black dhamma and how anandan breached the white breed white dhamma considered again 1 soul born in a high caste clan noble brahmin householder etc rich and who walks in the right way and after death he arises in heaven some of the white breed breed white dhamma and how ananda breeds the white breed nibana neither black nor white consider 1 so born a noble brahmin or householder etc he goes forth and rids himself of the 5 hindrances etc and he breeds nibana neither black nor white that's ananda some of the white breed breed nibana neither black nor white verily ananda these are the 6 breeds that's the end of the suta
commentary:
so here the buddha is talking about the 6 types of person 1 who comes from a black breed that means a bad a very low background born in a low caste flat clan as well as being very poor is also ugly looking etc and then if he walks the wrong way the ill way and then he ends up in the woeful plane so there's the black breed bringing black dhamma and on the other hand if he comes from a low-caste clan poor etc but he walks the good way in the 3 karmas of deed word and tortner then he arises in the heaven so he breeds white dhamma then the third 1 is of a low-caste clan but eventually he goes forth as a monk and cultivates him and attains arahanth so he breeds nibana similarly for the white breed and this suta is a little similar to another sort suta we saw earlier angutta nikaya 4.85 where the buddha talked about 4 persons found in the world 1 who is in darkness and bound for darkness 1 who is in darkness bound for light 1 who sees in lightner and bound for darkness 1 who is in light and bound for light so it's a bit similar to that suitable
